column1: JUST IN | The track for Debby remains on course. Winds are up to 65 mph. NEW this hour... the storm surge WARNING is now in effect from Longboat Key north, including Tampa Bay. The NHC has upped the forecast in these areas from a 2-4' surge, to a 3-5' surge.

Helpful ideas if anyone needs during times like this with tropical storms and hurricanes. If you have an Ice maker or you buy a bag of ice separate your ice into frozen Ziploc bags and place those in your fridge at the coldest temperature. Place frozen and quart size baggies in your drawers where you keep your cheeses or any type of produce to keep cold extra cold in case of power outage. I do this every year and it really helps if the power goes out your food will stay cold and you can use an ice chest too as well. Right now I have 3 frozen gallon size bag Ziploc in the middle section of my fridge since last night and the ice cubes are still frozen. Today I did the quart size for the drawers.so do as many as you need and zip them tight. I hope this helps anyone that needs it
good idea
Excellent tips! I also like to freeze small bottles of water. I take them out to drink, and they stay cold for a very long time. It's nice if you have yard cleanup to do, in particular.

We do this too! We freeze water bottles and if we lose power we move them to the fridge! We take a couple out to thaw to drink and swap them with more from the fridge. So we have water to drink and keep the fridge cold!

We hear this often .Here’s the thing .
1. We’re dealing with nature. There are no guarantees, no rules
2. Reports come originally from the National Hurricane Center. Predictions are made from the info they have at that precise moment.
3. We are told constantly by Denis and other weather people that these predictions are NOT written in stone and they are subject to change.
4. Old timers know to listen to the reports and act accordingly but they realize these are not guarantees. Not one single person reported otherwise ..
